name: CHERIoT — Microcontroller CHERI
summary: 32-bit RISC-V CHERI variant designed from scratch for MCU-class deeply embedded systems; combines hardware memory safety with rich compartmentalisation. CHERIoT 1.0 ISA released November 2025; SCI Semiconductors ICENI chip is the first commercial CHERI silicon, scaling production in 2026.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**CHERIoT** is a 32-bit RISC-V CHERI variant designed from scratch for MCU-class deeply embedded systems; combines hardware memory safety with rich compartmentalisation. Object-granularity heap safety in hardware (incl. assembly); revocation-based use-after-free; lightweight cross-compartment calls; 64-bit capability over 32-bit address (4 bytes metadata + 4 bytes addr).

CHERIoT is the most plausible near-term CHERI deployment: it avoids the Morello-class store-buffer cost by targeting MCUs where pointer-size growth is acceptable and compartmentalisation is the killer feature.

sections.title: Status and ICENI silicon
sections.content:
Status (as of 2026-04): CHERIoT 1.0 ISA released November 2025; SCI Semiconductors **ICENI** chip (cheriot-ibex core) returning from fab and scaling mass production in 2026 — *first commercial CHERI silicon*. Microsoft's CHERIoT-RTOS is open-source; CHERIoT-Rust port active.

The object-granularity revocation mechanism gives CHERIoT temporal safety in addition to the spatial safety inherited from CHERI's capabilities — addressing the use-after-free class that pure CHERI capabilities alone do not.
